Off Calendar Jun-28-2022 Continued To Aug-31-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-22-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. JAMES A. LASSART, Esq. and ADRIAN G. DRISCOLL, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) for plaintiff;
Atty. MICHAEL T. KENNEDY, JR. of Berding & Weil LLP (Tel: 925-838-2090) for defendants Christina McCandless and Principle Association Management, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAVANNAH HUDSON'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Savannah Hudson's motion to strike amended complaint is denied.

"In order to survive a motion to strike an allegation of punitive damages, the ultimate facts showing an entitlement to such relief must be pled by a plaintiff." (Clauson v. Superior Ct. (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 1253, 1255.) "[F]acts must be alleged in the pleading to support such a claim; the mere allegation that an intentional tort was committed is not sufficient." (Grieves v. Superior Ct. (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 166.)

Plaintiff pleads ultimate facts showing an entitlement to punitive damages for intentional torts. She does more than merely allege that an intentional tort was committed, but rather pleads facts showing that Defendant approached her and pushed her without provocation. These allegations are sufficient to survive a motion to strike. (Clauson, 67 Cal.App.4th at 1255 ["In passing on the correctness of a ruling on a motion to strike, judges read allegations of a pleading subject to a motion to strike as a whole, all parts in their context, and assume their truth."].)

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
CGC-21-592749 Joanna L. Pfeister vs. Christina McCandless et al and
CGC-21-593282 Joanna L. Pfeister vs. Savannah M. Hudson et al.

APPEARANCES:
Joanna Pfeister, (617.429.2355; J@Pfeister.com) Plaintiff in propria persona via Zoom Audio.
Michael T. Kennedy Jr. (925.838.2090; mkennedy@berdingweil.com) of Berding Weill for Defendant.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with parties' stipulations as discussed during hearing as follows:

DEFENDANT SAVANNAH HUDSON's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT:

Defendant Hudson's demurrer to plaintiff's Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend to plead ultimate facts to support causes of action (1) elder abuse, (3) intentional infliction of emotional distress, (5) assault, and (6) battery. A complaint must allege "facts constituting a cause of action, in ordinary and concise language." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 425.10(a)(1).) The gravamen of this action appears to be that Ms. Hudson assaulted plaintiff while plaintiff was attempting to use the restroom. Plaintiff has leave to plead ultimate facts regarding the alleged assault/battery. The current allegations, which merely reference vague e-mails regarding the alleged "assault," are conclusory and fail to state a viable claim.

The demurrer to cause of action (2) for violation of Penal Code sec. 368 is sustained without leave to amend. "A statute that provides for a criminal proceeding only does not create a civil liability; if there is no provision for a remedy by civil action to persons injured by a breach of the statute it is because the Legislature did not contemplate one." (Clinkscales v. Carver (1943) 22 Cal.2d 72, 75.) Plaintiff's remedy is to pursue her elder abuse claim under the Welfare and Institutions Code, which covers the same subject matter.

The demurrers to causes of action (4) fraud, (7) defamation, (8) slander, (9) Civil Code sec. 4935(b) [board adjourning executive session]; (10) Corporation Code sec. 8334 [director's rights], and (11) Corporation Code sec. 5047.5 [action against officers/directors] are sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to explain how she can plead facts to support these claims. As noted, this appears to be an assault/battery case. There is no clarification regarding how Ms. Hudson, the only defendant in this case, defrauded or defamed plaintiff. There is also no description how Ms. Hudson, a former property manager, can be liable for the purported violations of the Civil and Corporations Codes. (Amended Complaint, Ex. 1; see Mendoza v. JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A. (2016) 6 Cal.App.5th 802, 809 [plaintiff must explain how she can cure complaint defects to obtain leave to amend].)

Plaintiff failed to comply with her meet and confer obligations for a motion/demurrer attacking the pleadings. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 430.41; Kennedy, Jr. Decl. (10/15/21).) The Court orders the parties to engage in good faith meet and confer efforts for any future motion/demurrer attacking the pleadings. Failure to do so may result in court sanctions, including contempt.

Parties to discuss an approved form of order adopting the court's tentative ruling and to include parties' stipulation and email it to the court for review at contestdept302tr@sftc.org.
